verse: I am the door: by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and out, and find pasture.
meditations: He is the door, not the gatekeeper.
Any man may enter. That includes you.
Entering is how you are saved.
The way in is a Person.
You go in and out freely.
Pasture is on the other side of Him.
The door is open, not guarded against you.
You do not climb. You enter.
He named Himself the way in.
Salvation is by Him, not by effort.
Find pasture means He feeds you.
There is no entrance exam.
He made access simple on purpose.
In and out speaks of freedom, not probation.
The door does not close behind you.

verse: I am the good shepherd, and know my sheep, and am known of mine.
meditations: He is the good shepherd.
He knows His sheep by name.
You are known, not merely counted.
Being known is better than being noticed.
He knows you and stays anyway.
The shepherd is good, so the sheep are safe.
You know His voice because He speaks.
Nothing about you is unknown to Him.
He is not a hireling.
Good means He will not leave.
You are His, and He says so.
Knowing runs both directions.
He does not lose track of you.
The relationship is personal.
You belong to a good shepherd.

verse: As the Father knoweth me, even so know I the Father: and I lay down my life for the sheep.
meditations: He laid down His life for the sheep.
The shepherd died for the flock.
This was chosen, not suffered by accident.
He knew the cost before He came.
For the sheep. For you.
Love proved itself by dying.
The Father knows Him. He knows you.
He gave His life, not merely His time.
You were worth the laying down.
The good shepherd does not flee.
His death was deliberate love.
He stood between you and the wolf.
Laid down speaks of willingness.
You are the reason He stayed.
The price was paid by the shepherd.

verse: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me:
meditations: My sheep hear my voice.
He knows them. He knows you.
You can recognize Him because you are His.
Following comes from belonging.
His voice is familiar to His own.
You are not straining to be noticed.
I know them is His claim over you.
Hearing is evidence, not effort.
The sheep follow because they trust.
He speaks in a voice you know.
You are counted among His sheep.
Knowing you is not conditional.
The relationship begins on His side.
You follow because He called first.
His voice will not mislead you.

verse: Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also.
meditations: Because I live, ye shall live also.
His life guarantees yours.
Your future is tied to His.
He lives. That settles the matter.
You see Him when the world cannot.
Life is not something you sustain.
The resurrection is your warranty.
Because He lives is the whole reason.
You shall live also. Not maybe.
His life is the source of yours.
Death did not hold Him, or you.
You are joined to a living Christ.
The little while ends in seeing Him.
His life is the reason you have one.
You live because He does.

verse: Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.
meditations: Now ye are clean.
Clean already, not clean eventually.
The word He spoke made you clean.
You did not scrub your way here.
Cleansing came by His speaking.
Now is the tense. Not soon.
You are not waiting to qualify.
His word does what it says.
Clean is your present condition.
He declared it, so it is true.
You are not on a cleaning schedule.
The word did the work.
Nothing unclean remains between you and Him.
He calls you clean. Believe Him.
Cleanness was given, not earned.

verse: As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you: continue ye in my love.
meditations: As the Father loved me, so I loved you.
The measure is the Father's love for the Son.
You are loved with that same love.
Not a lesser love. The same one.
Continue in it. It is already yours.
His love is not on a sliding scale.
You were loved before you responded.
The Father's love is the standard.
So have I loved you is a finished statement.
You live inside that love now.
There is nowhere higher to be loved from.
Continue means it is already surrounding you.
He loves you as the Father loves Him.
The love is eternal in origin.
You are loved perfectly.

verse: Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.
meditations: Greater love hath no man than this.
He laid down His life for His friends.
This is the ceiling of love, and He reached it.
You are called friend, not servant.
No greater love exists to look for.
He proved it rather than declared it.
Friendship cost Him everything.
The greatest love was aimed at you.
Nothing more could have been given.
He did not withhold Himself.
You are on the receiving end of the greatest love.
Laying down was voluntary.
His friends were worth His life to Him.
This love has already been demonstrated.
There is no love above this one.

verse: Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy.
meditations: Your sorrow shall be turned into joy.
Turned, not merely replaced.
The weeping is real and it is temporary.
He does not deny the sorrow.
Joy is the destination of this grief.
Sorrow has an expiry date.
He promised the turning Himself.
The same sorrow becomes the joy.
You are not wrong to be grieving now.
Lament is allowed here.
The turning is His work, not yours.
Weeping does not get the last word.
Your grief is going somewhere.
He said shall, not might.
Joy is coming, and He is bringing it.

verse: I have glorified thee on the earth: I have finished the work which thou gavest me to do.
meditations: I have finished the work.
The work was completed by Him.
Nothing was left half done.
You are not finishing what He started.
Finished is the word He chose.
The task the Father gave was accomplished.
There is no remaining balance.
He glorified God by completing it.
Rest where He said finished.
Your effort was never required here.
The work is behind Him, and you.
He did not leave it to you.
Completion belongs to Christ.
You inherit a finished work.
Nothing about your salvation is pending.

verse: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in thy name: those that thou gavest me I have kept, and none of them is lost, but the son of perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled.
meditations: Those the Father gave, He kept.
None of them is lost.
He does the keeping.
You are held, not holding on.
Kept in the Father's name.
His record of keeping is perfect.
You were given to Him.
Losing you is not among the outcomes.
The keeping does not depend on your grip.
He guards what He was given.
Not one was lost. Not one will be.
You are safe in His keeping.
He kept them while He was with them.
Being given to Christ is being secured.
Your safety is His responsibility.

verse: Father, I will that they also, whom thou hast given me, be with me where I am; that they may behold my glory, which thou hast given me: for thou lovedst me before the foundation of the world.
meditations: He wants you with Him where He is.
Loved before the foundation of the world.
The love predates creation.
He asked the Father for your company.
Being with Him is His desire, not only yours.
You will behold His glory.
The Father loved the Son before time.
That ancient love now includes you.
He prayed for you to be there.
Your place with Him is requested by Him.
Before the world was, love already existed.
He wills that you be with Him.
You are wanted, not merely permitted.
The destination is His presence.
His prayer will be answered.

verse: And I have declared unto them thy name, and will declare it: that the love wherewith thou hast loved me may be in them, and I in them.
meditations: The love the Father has for the Son is in you.
He declared the Father's name so you would know it.
That love may be in them, and I in them.
You carry the love the Son receives.
Christ in you is the point.
The Father's love was transferred, not diluted.
He will keep declaring it.
You are indwelt by love itself.
This is the love He has always known.
In them means inside you.
The same love, the same warmth.
He wanted you to have what He has.
You are not outside that circle.
His name is made known to you.
Love and Christ dwell in the same place.

verse: And this is the Father’s will which hath sent me, that of all which he hath given me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up again at the last day.
meditations: He should lose nothing.
The Father's will is that you be kept.
Raising you up is part of the plan.
Nothing given to Christ is mislaid.
The last day holds no threat for you.
You are included in all which He gave.
Losing you would break the Father's will.
He keeps what He was given.
Your security rests on His obedience.
Nothing is the amount He will lose.
The resurrection is promised, not hoped.
You will be raised up.
His track record is complete.
This is the Father's will, not a preference.
You cannot fall out of His hands.

verse: And the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, This man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.
meditations: This man receiveth sinners.
They meant it as an accusation. It is good news.
He eats with them, not merely tolerates them.
Sinners drew near to hear Him.
Being a sinner is the qualification.
He receives. He does not screen.
The table is where He meets them.
Your record does not repel Him.
He was known for welcoming the wrong people.
The murmur was true.
You are exactly who He receives.
Nearness was possible for publicans.
He did not wait for them to reform.
Receiving sinners is His reputation.
Come as you actually are.

verse: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry.
meditations: This my son was dead, and is alive again.
Lost, and is found.
The father calls him son before he cleans up.
Restoration came before explanation.
He was received at the gate, not after review.
Alive again is the father's verdict.
The celebration was the father's idea.
You are not on trial coming home.
Found means Someone was looking.
The rehearsed speech was interrupted.
He is glad you came back.
Dead and alive describe the same person.
Sonship was never revoked.
The feast begins at your return.
You are welcomed, not merely readmitted.

verse: And he said unto him, Son, thou art ever with me, and all that I have is thine.
meditations: Son, thou art ever with me.
All that I have is thine.
The father went out to the angry one too.
You already have everything.
Nearness was never withdrawn.
He intreated the son who would not come in.
Resentment did not end the relationship.
Ever with me means always.
You have not been overlooked.
The inheritance was never in doubt.
He calls him son while he sulks.
The father comes out to both sons.
All that I have is not a small offer.
You do not need to earn the estate.
He addresses your bitterness gently.

verse: For all these things do the nations of the world seek after: and your Father knoweth that ye have need of these things.
meditations: Your Father knoweth that ye have need.
He knows before you explain.
Need is not news to Him.
You have a Father, not a supplier.
The nations seek. Children are known.
Knowing precedes providing.
He is aware of what you lack.
Your Father is the one who knows.
Anxiety adds nothing to His awareness.
He has already taken account.
You are not informing God.
Fatherhood is the reason for confidence.
He knows, and He is good.
Your needs are held by Him.
Nothing you need has escaped Him.

verse: Speak ye comfortably to Jerusalem, and cry unto her, that her warfare is accomplished, that her iniquity is pardoned: for she hath received of the Lord’s hand double for all her sins.
meditations: Her warfare is accomplished.
Her iniquity is pardoned.
Speak comfortably. That is the instruction.
The conflict has been completed.
Pardon is announced, not negotiated.
God tells others to comfort you.
The sentence has been served.
Accomplished means it is behind you.
Comfort is the message He sends.
Your warfare has an ending.
Iniquity pardoned is a legal fact.
He wants you told plainly.
The long struggle is declared over.
God speaks tenderly on purpose.
Pardoned is what He calls you.

verse: Remember these, O Jacob and Israel; for thou art my servant: I have formed thee; thou art my servant: O Israel, thou shalt not be forgotten of me.
meditations: Thou shalt not be forgotten of me.
He formed you. He remembers you.
Forgetting you is not possible for Him.
You are His servant, and He says so.
Remember these means He has not moved.
He formed thee is past and permanent.
Being forgotten is your fear, not His plan.
He speaks your name to remind you.
Not forgotten, even when you feel it.
His memory does not fail.
You were made by Him deliberately.
He claims you twice in one verse.
The relationship holds when feeling does not.
You are remembered right now.
He will not forget you.

verse: Let the wicked forsake his way, and the unrighteous man his thoughts: and let him return unto the Lord, and he will have mercy upon him; and to our God, for he will abundantly pardon.
meditations: He will abundantly pardon.
Return, and mercy meets you.
Abundantly is the manner of His pardon.
Mercy is promised to the one who turns.
He will have mercy. Not might.
The way back is open.
Pardon is not measured out sparingly.
Your thoughts can be forsaken and forgiven.
God is generous with forgiveness.
Returning is welcomed, not penalized.
Abundant pardon leaves nothing outstanding.
He does not forgive reluctantly.
Mercy is waiting on the other side of turning.
The invitation includes the unrighteous.
Come back. He will pardon abundantly.

verse: And in that day ye shall ask me nothing. Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he will give it you.
meditations: Ask the Father in my name.
His name is what gives you access.
You do not approach on your own credit.
He will give it you.
Prayer runs through Christ's name.
The Father hears because of the Son.
You are welcome to ask.
In my name means with His standing.
Access was arranged by Jesus.
Asking is invited, not tolerated.
The Father is willing to give.
You come with Christ's credentials.
Verily, verily. He means it.
The door to the Father is open.
Ask, because He said to.

verse: At that day ye shall know that I am in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.
meditations: Ye in me, and I in you.
The union is mutual.
You are inside Christ.
Christ is inside you.
At that day ye shall know it.
This is position, not feeling.
He is in the Father, and you in Him.
The chain reaches from God to you.
You are not outside the life of God.
Knowing comes after the fact is true.
Union was made, not maintained by you.
Nothing separates what is joined here.
In me is where you live now.
He does not dwell at a distance.
You and Christ share one life.

verse: And the glory which thou gavest me I have given them; that they may be one, even as we are one:
meditations: The glory the Father gave Him, He gave you.
Given, not lent.
He shares what He received.
Glory was handed down to you.
That they may be one, as we are one.
You hold something He was given.
Christ did not keep it to Himself.
Oneness is the purpose of the giving.
You are included in the Son's inheritance.
The glory is already given.
He was generous with what was His.
You share in what belongs to Christ.
Unity comes from a gift, not effort.
What the Father gave, the Son passed on.
You have been given glory.

verse: And this is the will of him that sent me, that every one which seeth the Son, and believeth on him, may have everlasting life: and I will raise him up at the last day.
meditations: Everyone who sees and believes has everlasting life.
This is the will of the Father.
I will raise him up at the last day.
Everlasting life is present tense.
Seeing the Son is where it begins.
The promise is to every one, not a few.
He commits to raising you up.
May have everlasting life. Not might.
Believing is the whole condition.
The Father willed your rescue.
You are among the everyone.
The last day is a promise, not a threat.
Christ guarantees the outcome Himself.
Life that lasts is given, not achieved.
He will raise you up.
